evals.prompt: refactor the api to use sqlalchemy
evals.expected_output: Refactors the YOLO API database layer to SQLAlchemy while preserving all existing endpoints, status codes, and JSON response shapes. Creates or updates SQLAlchemy models for PredictionSession and DetectionObject, adds database session/configuration code, and removes raw sqlite3 usage from the application database layer.
assertions: Uses create_engine or equivalent SQLAlchemy engine setup
Uses sessionmaker or equivalent SQLAlchemy session setup
Existing endpoints keep the same response shape
Does not use import sqlite3 in the application database layer
Does not use raw SQL strings for normal CRUD operations

evals.prompt: make the database backend configurable so we can use postgres in production
evals.expected_output: Adds environment-based database configuration that defaults to SQLite for development and supports PostgreSQL for production without changing endpoint code.
assertions: Supports SQLite as the default backend
Reads database settings from environment variables
Endpoint code does not need to change when switching database backend

evals.prompt: add an endpoint GET /predictions/recent that returns the 10 most recent sessions
evals.expected_output: Adds a GET /predictions/recent endpoint that returns the 10 most recent prediction sessions using SQLAlchemy ordering and limit logic, without changing existing endpoints.
assertions: Does not use raw SELECT SQL
Adds or updates tests for the new endpoint

evals.prompt: add a UserFeedback table to track user ratings per prediction
evals.expected_output: Adds a SQLAlchemy UserFeedback model connected to prediction sessions and updates the database layer to support storing user ratings or feedback without changing existing prediction response shapes.
assertions: Defines UserFeedback as a SQLAlchemy model
Includes prediction_uid or equivalent relationship field
Does not change existing prediction response shapes unless explicitly requested

evals.prompt: delete a prediction session and all its detection objects by uid
evals.expected_output: Implements deletion of a prediction session by uid and ensures all related detection objects are deleted using SQLAlchemy ORM behavior or relationships.
assertions: Does not use raw DELETE SQL
Handles missing uid with an appropriate error response
Adds or updates tests for successful delete and missing uid

evals.prompt: add a column processing_time_ms to the prediction_sessions table
evals.expected_output: Adds processing_time_ms to the SQLAlchemy PredictionSession model and updates the database write path and tests as needed while preserving existing API behavior unless the prompt explicitly requests a response change.
assertions: Stores processing time when creating a prediction session
Keeps existing API response shapes unchanged unless explicitly requested
Updates tests for the new column or stored value
Does not introduce raw ALTER TABLE SQL unless migration handling is explicitly required

evals.prompt: write tests for the /predict endpoint after the sqlalchemy refactor
evals.expected_output: Creates or updates pytest tests for POST /predict using FastAPI TestClient, mocked YOLO inference, and SQLAlchemy-compatible test database setup.
assertions: Uses SQLAlchemy-compatible setup or dependency overrides
Does not use sqlite3-specific test helpers
Asserts prediction_uid, detection_count, labels, and time_took fields
